paper_metadata.title: Multitask Prompted Training Enables Zero-Shot Task Generalization
paper_metadata.abstract: Large language models have recently been shown to attain reasonable zero-shot generalization on a diverse set of tasks (Brown et al., 2020). It has been hypothesized that this is a consequence of implicit multitask learning in language models' pretraining (Radford et al., 2019). Can zero-shot generalization instead be directly induced by explicit multitask learning? To test this question at scale, we develop a system for easily mapping any natural language tasks into a human-readable prompted form. We convert a large set of supervised datasets, each with multiple prompts with diverse wording. These prompted datasets allow for benchmarking the ability of a model to perform completely held-out tasks. We fine-tune a pretrained encoder-decoder model (Raffel et al., 2020; Lester et al., 2021) on this multitask mixture covering a wide variety of tasks. The model attains strong zero-shot performance on several standard datasets, often outperforming models up to 16x its size. Further, our approach attains strong performance on a subset of tasks from the BIG-bench benchmark, outperforming models up to 6x its size. All trained models are available at https://github.com/bigscience-workshop/t-zero and all prompts are available at https://github.com/bigscience-workshop/promptsource.

extraction_data.innovation: The key innovation is multitask prompted training, where a pre-trained encoder-decoder model is fine-tuned on a large mixture of natural language tasks formatted as prompts, enabling strong zero-shot generalization on held-out tasks.
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, zero-shot task transfer, question answering, sentiment analysis, summarization, natural language inference
extraction_data.paper_title: Multitask Prompted Training Enables Zero-Shot Task Generalization

extraction_data.innovation: Multitask prompted training enables zero-shot task generalization by training a single model on a diverse set of tasks using natural language prompts, allowing it to generalize to unseen tasks without task-specific fine-tuning.
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Masked language modeling (MLM) with span corruption
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Prompted training for zero-shot task generalization
extraction_data.extension: Uses a unified framework of natural language prompts to represent diverse tasks, enabling zero-shot transfer across 100+ tasks.
extraction_data.research_problem: Zero-shot task generalization in natural language processing
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, question answering, text generation, classification, summarization, and other NLP tasks
extraction_data.paper_title: Multitask Prompted Training Enables Zero-Shot Task Generalization
